title: CMMC Ecosystem
core_principle: The CMMC ecosystem is a structured network of organizations and individuals with defined roles, responsibilities, and relationships. Knowing who does what and who they answer to is what this domain tests.

governing_authorities.name: Office of the Undersecretary of Defense (OUSD)
governing_authorities.role: The DoD authority responsible for CMMC policy. Establishes cybersecurity standards and best practices and maps controls across CMMC levels. Responsible for DFARS 252.204-7012 which created the original verification component for contractor cybersecurity requirements.

governing_authorities.name: CMMC Accreditation Body (CMMC-AB)
governing_authorities.role: The independent accreditation body that oversees the CMMC ecosystem. Accredits C3PAOs, certifies assessors and instructors, and maintains the integrity of the certification program.

governing_authorities.name: CMMC Assessors and Instructors Certification Organization (CAICO)
governing_authorities.role: Part of the CMMC-AB structure. Oversees certification of individual assessors and instructors including CCPs, CCAs, and CCIs.

organizations.definition: A defense contractor seeking CMMC certification to be eligible for DoD contracts that require it.
organizations.purpose: Must achieve the appropriate CMMC level to be awarded contracts requiring that certification.

organizations.definition: An accredited organization authorized to conduct official CMMC Level 2 certification assessments.
organizations.purpose: Performs third-party assessments on OSCs, reviews assessment reports before submission to SPRS, and issues Certificates of CMMC Status.
organizations.key_fact: C3PAOs are accredited by the CMMC-AB. Without C3PAO accreditation an organization cannot conduct official CMMC assessments.

organizations.definition: An organization registered with the CMMC-AB to provide CMMC consulting and implementation services to OSCs.
organizations.purpose: Employs Registered Practitioners (RPs) who help contractors prepare for CMMC assessments. RPOs advise and implement — they do not assess.
organizations.key_distinction: RPOs help OSCs get ready. C3PAOs assess whether they are ready.

organizations.definition: Organizations licensed to develop and publish CMMC training content.
organizations.purpose: Create the curriculum and materials used by Licensed Training Providers.

organizations.definition: Organizations licensed to deliver CMMC training including the mandatory CCP preparatory course.
organizations.purpose: Deliver training to candidates seeking CMMC certifications. Completing an LTP course is a prerequisite for the CCP exam.

individuals.definition: Individual registered with the CMMC-AB who works for an RPO providing CMMC consulting services.
individuals.role: Advises, consults, and makes recommendations to clients. Helps implement security controls. Does NOT participate in certified CMMC assessments.
individuals.key_distinction: RPs are implementers and consultants. They are not assessors.

individuals.definition: Individual who has passed the CCP exam and is certified to assist on CMMC assessment teams.
individuals.role: Assists the assessment team during CMMC assessments. Cannot lead assessments or make independent scoring determinations.
individuals.pathway: CCP is the entry-level assessment credential. Required before pursuing CCA certification.
individuals.key_fact: CCP certification is a prerequisite for CCA and CCI certifications.

individuals.definition: Individual certified to conduct CMMC assessments as a full assessment team member.
individuals.role: Conducts assessments, makes scoring determinations. Works under the Lead Assessor.

individuals.definition: Individual certified to teach CMMC courses at Licensed Training Providers.
individuals.role: Delivers official CMMC training including CCP preparatory courses.

individuals.definition: Senior certified individual who leads CMMC assessment teams.
individuals.role: Leads the assessment, makes final scoring decisions, drafts and submits the final assessment report.
individuals.key_fact: The Lead Assessor is the only person who can submit the final assessment report.

individuals.definition: Legacy credential from early CMMC program. Being sunset as CCP and CCA credentials mature.
individuals.status: Sunsetting — being replaced by CCP/CCA pathway.

individuals.definition: Legacy credential from early CMMC program. Being sunset as CCI credential matures.
individuals.status: Sunsetting — being replaced by CCI pathway.

roles.CCP: Assists. Cannot lead or make independent determinations.
roles.CCA: Assesses. Makes scoring determinations under Lead Assessor.
roles.Lead_Assessor: Leads. Makes final decisions. Submits report.

assessment_team_structure.key_rule: CCP assists. CCA assesses. Lead Assessor leads.

ecosystem_relationships.who_conducts_Level2_assessments: C3PAOs using teams of CCPs and CCAs led by Lead Assessors

flashcards.question: What is the difference between an RPO and a C3PAO?
flashcards.answer: RPOs help OSCs prepare for assessments — they advise, consult, and implement. C3PAOs conduct the official assessments. RPOs get you ready, C3PAOs determine if you are ready.

flashcards.question: What can a Registered Practitioner NOT do?
flashcards.answer: RPs cannot participate in certified CMMC assessments. They are implementers and consultants only.

flashcards.question: What credential must you hold before pursuing CCA or CCI certification?
flashcards.answer: CCP — Certified CMMC Professional. It is a prerequisite for both CCA and CCI.

flashcards.question: What is the difference between CCP, CCA, and Lead Assessor on an assessment team?
flashcards.answer: CCP assists. CCA assesses and makes scoring determinations. Lead Assessor leads the team, makes final scoring decisions, and submits the final report.

flashcards.question: Who accredits C3PAOs?
flashcards.answer: The CMMC-AB — Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification Accreditation Body.

flashcards.question: What is the difference between an LPP and an LTP?
flashcards.answer: LPPs develop and publish training content. LTPs deliver that training to candidates. LPPs create the curriculum, LTPs teach it.

flashcards.question: Who conducts Level 3 CMMC assessments?
flashcards.answer: DCMA DIBCAC — the Defense Contract Management Agency's Defense Industrial Base Cybersecurity Assessment Center.

flashcards.question: What are Provisional Assessors and Provisional Instructors?
flashcards.answer: Legacy credentials from the early CMMC program that are being sunset as the CCP/CCA/CCI pathway matures.

flashcards.question: What is an OSC?
flashcards.answer: Organization Seeking Certification — a defense contractor pursuing CMMC certification to be eligible for DoD contracts requiring it. Also called OSA (Organization Seeking Assessment).

flashcards.question: What is the OUSD's role in the CMMC ecosystem?
flashcards.answer: The DoD authority responsible for CMMC policy. Establishes cybersecurity standards, maps controls across levels, and is responsible for DFARS 252.204-7012.
